Title: Nunu
Age: Current9 years old Timeline Born988 AN Edit

children.title: Life as a Notai
children.textContent:
Born into the wandering Notai tribe of the Freljord, Nunu grew up surrounded by music, storytelling, and celebration. His mother, Layka, taught him that every person, place, and moment carried a story worth singing. Together they traveled from village to village, sharing songs of ancient heroes and spreading joy before each winter’s arrival.

On his fifth nameday, Layka gifted Nunu a flute, that he later christened Svellsongur , so he could learn to play her melodies himself. Several peaceful years went by until raiders attacked the Notai caravan, separating Nunu from his mother. Rescued by the surviving Notai, he waits for weeks for his mother to return.

As time goes by Nunu starts to isolate himself more and more from the other Notai survivors, escaping into the legends and heroes of old he heard in his mother’s songs. Determined to become one of these heroes, Nunu set his sights on a fierce monster that killed all who sought its power, thwarting the local hunters who were sent each year, never to return. Using his flute, Nunu slipped away from camp atop a herd of elkyr.

children.title: Meeting Willump
children.textContent:
On his quest to prove himself a true hero, Nunu wandered into the forgotten ruins of an ancient yeti civilization. Deep within, he encountered a giant creature standing over a glowing gem. The Yeti, looming out of the ice with fangs and claws, didn’t register to Nunu as danger the way others would. In his mind, this was the moment every great hero faced: the mighty beast guarding ancient power. He imagined dragons, serpents, and the champions of old, and the magic in the glowing gem responded, letting those visions swirl around him. But when he looked at the creature more closely. Instead of a fearsome foe, he saw something confused, lonely, and in need of someone to play with. The creature, still enraged from centuries of isolation, did not expect the first snowball, or the second. Their encounter shifted rapidly into a full snowball fight. As they tumbled and threw snow, Nunu noticed the guardian’s appearance and demeanor changing. The creature grew progressively furrier and friendlier. Its harsh growl gradually shifted into something like laughter.

The moment broke when the guardian accidentally snapped Nunu’s flute, his last connection to his mother. As Nunu cried over his broken flute, the guardian realized that the future of the Freljord didn’t depend on the gem itself, but on the child who could shape its power. With a gesture from the Yeti, the magic flowed into Nunu, allowing him to bring his imagination to life and repairing his flute, freezing it into True Ice. With his new Yeti friend now named "Willump" in tow, they set out across the Freljord, chasing his mother's songs while holding onto the hope of finding her.

children.title: Traveling to the Winged Mountain
children.textContent:
During their voyage, Nunu awakens from a dream of his mother, Layka, and sets out with his yeti companion, Willump, to find the Heart of the Blue, a mysterious artifact said to reside in the Winged Mountain and the key to reuniting him with his mother. Along the way, they battle ice wolves covered in a strange purple ice and discover a cave of True Ice, partially corrupted by the Void. By purifying the statue of Anivia at its center, they unlock a passage and continue their journey.

During their travels, Nunu and Willump set up camp and share moments of music and remembrance of Nunu’s mother. Nunu dreams of Layka, who speaks cryptically about the Heart of the Blue as a weapon of light and warmth against darkness, while Lissandra secretly observes and seeks to exploit the artifact to control True Ice and combat the Void.

Their quest brings them to meet Braum who saves them from a pack of ice wolves and who helps guide them through a fierce blizzard that blocks the way to the Winged Mountain. In search of a cauldron to prepare hero stew, they encounter Ornn in his forge and complete a series of trials to gather rare components in order for Ornn to forge the cauldron. Inside the Winged Mountain, they encounter the Volibear, who tests their mettle and shows them the way to Ramshara, the heart of the mountain and the ancestral home of the Yeti.

children.title: The Truth of Ramshara
children.textContent:
As Nunu and Willump reach Ramshara, they discover a city frozen in time. Through murals and spirits, they uncover the history of the yetis: once feral creatures, they were blessed by Anivia to gain additional arms and the power to create True Ice. The yetis fought alongside the Three Sisters against the Void, but Lissandra betrayed them, draining their magic to build her True Ice wall and sacrificing their army, including her own sisters, to secure victory.

Willump, the sole surviving yeti, reveals he painted the murals and has been guarding the Heart of the Blue. Nunu helps free him from a trance caused by lingering spirits, and learns that the Heart of the Blue is a part of Anivia’s essence, kept safe by Willump. Meanwhile, Lissandra manipulates Nunu’s mother in his dreams to probe for the artifact’s location. When Willump is taken by Lissandra as her ultimate weapon, Nunu refuses to abandon him, and with the help of Braum, begins planning a rescue.

children.title: Escape from the Frostguard
children.textContent:
Nunu, aided by Braum, infiltrates the Frostguard Citadel to rescue Willump. After freeing his friend, the duo escapes across icy terrain, using Willump’s magic to overcome the Frostguard. Lissandra confronts them, attempting to turn Willump against Nunu, but Nunu’s courage and the guidance of his mother’s spirit help him reach his friend. Together, they counter Lissandra’s attacks, purify Willump, and escape, with Nunu realizing that the true Heart of the Blue is the bond he shares with Willump.

Afterward, Braum departs to follow his own path, while Nunu and Willump test their newfound powers and prepare for future adventures. Lissandra, left to reflect, realizes that Nunu, not herself, is destined to be the savior of the Freljord and resolves to observe and influence him from afar.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Nunu is a small Notai boy of eight years, with tanned skin, dark hair and blue eyes. He wears a orange furred cape, with leather gloves and boots, and a hood made in semblance of Willump.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent:
Nunu is a kind, excitable child, eager for adventures and travelling. Having listened to the stories of his mother since he was born, Nunu dreams of being a mighty hero, fighting against terrible foes and exploring the world.

After the loss of his tribe, Nunu felt great sorrow, but nevertheless remained hopeful. Desiring to help his new caretaker Lissandra, and wishing to fullfill his dreams of being a hero, Nunu went to face a terrible beast and prove himself a hero for his people—only to meet Willump and befriend the yeti, the two forming an intense and loving bond.

Having been protected by Willump for a long time, Nunu is somewhat sheltered from the more complicated and darker aspects of life. He tends to see the best of people, for better or worse, and tries to fit them into his very naive beliefs about the world. Nunu also has a hyperactive imagination, often coming up with fantastic ideas to entertain himself and Willump.

children.title: Layka
children.textContent: Nunu’s mother, Layka, is the heart of his childhood and the source of nearly every story, dream, and song that guides him. After being separated from her during a raid, Nunu clings to her memory and melodies as his guiding light. Even when Lissandra manipulates her image, Layka’s true presence, whether in dreams or spirit, helps Nunu stay brave, kind, and true to himself.

children.title: Willump
children.textContent: Willump is Nunu’s closest friend, protector, and the family he found when he had none left. Their bond quickly deepened into a partnership forged through trust, laughter, and shared imagination. Willump guards Nunu just as fiercely as Nunu brings joy and purpose back into Willump’s long-isolated existence.

children.title: Anivia
children.textContent: Anivia is a spiritual guide to the Notai tribe from which Nunu hails. While he has never met her directly, her legends, taught to him by Layka, shaped his understanding of her importance to the land. During his journey, Nunu discovers that the Heart of the Blue is a shard of Anivia’s essence, deepening his connection to the Cryophoenix and placing him within her legacy of protecting the Freljord.

children.title: Braum
children.textContent: Nunu has always seen Braum as something to aspire toward. Meeting the legendary hero in person only strengthens that admiration. Braum treats Nunu with kindness, warmth, and respect, recognizing courage beyond the boy’s size. Their brief adventure together leaves Nunu with a greater understanding of what it means to be a true hero.

children.title: Lissandra
children.textContent: Lissandra seeks to control the power within Nunu and Willump. Though she initially tries to turn Willump against him and uses visions of his mother to probe his heart, Nunu ultimately resists her influence, refusing to sacrifice his closest friend. After their initial battle concludes and Nunu is able to heal her eyes for a moment, Lissandra starts to believe that Nunu, not her, may hold the true power to save the Freljord, and chooses to monitor the boy from a distance.

children.title: Ornn
children.textContent: Nunu first meets Ornn while searching for a cauldron to make his “hero stew,” stumbling into Hearth-Home with Willump. Although Ornn is initially irritated by the intrusion, he agrees to help, on the condition that Nunu proves his worth by completing a series of trials. Through these trials, Ornn witnesses Nunu’s potential and acknowledges the heavy burden the young boy will have to carry.

children.title: Volibear
children.textContent: Nunu encounters Valhir within the Winged Mountain, where the great spirit initially assails them in a storm of fury. Once his rage subsides, he tests both Nunu and Willump, urging them to prove their strength. In an earnest attempt to meet the challenge, Nunu tries to mimic Valhir’s thunderous roar, an effort that greatly amuses the demigod. The Volibear ultimately acknowledges their determination and allows them to continue toward Ramshara.

mainContent.textContent:
